svg.text: PERSIAN (فارسی) / KURDISH (کوردی)
IRANIAN BRANCH · HISTORY & LEGACY
PLATE 23D ALT · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— PERSIAN: HISTORY OF A WORLD LITERARY LANGUAGE —
THREE EMPIRES, ONE LANGUAGE
Achaemenid Persian (550–330 BCE): Old Persian inscriptions at Persepolis
Sasanian Persian (224–651 CE): Middle Persian / Pahlavi; Avestan sacred texts
New Persian (900 CE–present): Arabic script adopted; Samanid cultural renaissance
PERSIAN AS COURT LANGUAGE OF THE ISLAMIC WORLD
Persian became the prestige literary language of the Islamic world 900–1800 CE
Mughal India: official court language was Persian, not Hindi or Urdu
Ottoman court: poets wrote Persian · Central Asia: Persian = high culture
Persian was to the Islamic world what Latin was to medieval Europe
MODERN PERSIAN: THREE STANDARDS
Farsi: spoken in Iran · Dari: spoken in Afghanistan · Tajik: spoken in Tajikistan
Same language, three names, three scripts (Arabic/Arabic/Cyrillic)
~80M native speakers; ~130M total including L2
SURVIVAL PHRASE (PERSIAN)
In baraye shoma, azadaneh, az taraf kasani ke be ayandeh negar budand, sakhteh shodeh.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who looked toward the future."
(ayandeh = future; negar = looking; sakhteh shodeh = was made)
KURDISH — HISTORY: THE NATION WITHOUT A STATE
— KURDISH: HISTORY OF DISPERSAL & RESISTANCE —
WHO ARE THE KURDS? THE LARGEST STATELESS NATION
~30-40M Kurds: largest ethnic group in the world without a recognized state
Distributed across: Turkey (~15M) · Iran (~8M) · Iraq (~6M) · Syria (~3M) · diaspora
Kurdish homeland: the Zagros Mountains — same mountains where agriculture began
THE MEDES: FIRST KURDISH EMPIRE (~678–550 BCE)
Median Empire: possibly the first Iranian-speaking state; capital at Ecbatana
Medes conquered Assyria (612 BCE) with Babylonian allies
Conquered by Cyrus the Great (~550 BCE) — absorbed into Achaemenid Persia
TREATY OF SEVRES (1920) & ITS BETRAYAL
1920 Treaty of Sevres: promised an independent Kurdish state after WWI
1923 Treaty of Lausanne: replaced Sevres — Kurdish state removed from text
Kurdish uprisings in Turkey 1925, 1930, 1937–38; all suppressed violently
Kurdish language banned in Turkey 1924–1991 — could not legally teach or publish
IRAQI KURDISTAN: THE CLOSEST THING TO A KURDISH STATE
Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG) since 1992: autonomous in northern Iraq
Erbil (Hawler): capital; ~1M people; Kurdish-majority, internationally recognized
Peshmerga: Kurdish militia fought ISIS 2014–17; key US ally
KURDISH LANGUAGE TODAY
Kurmanji: Turkish Kurdistan + northern Syria — written in Latin since 1932
Sorani: Iraqi Kurdistan + Iranian Kurdistan — written in Arabic-based script
Kurdish-language media: Rudaw TV, Kurdistan24 — broadcasting in Sorani and Kurmanji
SURVIVAL PHRASE (KURDISH / KURMANJI)
Ev ji bo te hatiye amade kirin, belaş, ji aliye kesane ku li ber xwe dinerin.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who look forward."
(belaş = free/gratis; li ber xwe dinerin = looking forward)

THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
